subject: [PATCH 2/2] btrfs: test qgroup deletion races with squota writes

reviews.author: Anand Jain
reviews.summary:
The reviewer confirmed that the required btrfs squota patches were present in the kernel and verified that the test case passes after a fresh restart, indicating that the issue was likely due to a messed-up VM image.

The reviewer asked if the year in the copyright notice should be updated from 2024 to 2026.
reviews.raw_body:
Boris,


The 7.1.0-rc3-gf5d9f327e4b2 kernel includes the for-next tree
plus my latest f_fsid kernel patches.


I double-checked and the required btrfs squota patches were
indeed present:

$ git log --oneline fs/btrfs | grep "check for subvolume before"
404bf5a24f2e btrfs: check for subvolume before deleting squota qgroup
$ git log --oneline fs/btrfs | grep "forbid creating subvol qgroups"
0c309d66dacd btrfs: forbid creating subvol qgroups

Possibly the issue was likely my messed-up VM image from
SELinux + fanotifywait troubleshooting last evening.

After a fresh restart, btrfs/348 passes:

$ ./check -s gen btrfs/348
...
Ran: btrfs/348
Passed all 1 tests

Sorry for the noise!.

Looks good.


Reviewed-by: Anand Jain <asj@kernel.org>

A small nit (which can be fixed during merge).

---

Do you need this to be   `s/2024/2026/`  ?

Thanks.

2026-05-19.patch_summary: This patch adds four test cases to the Btrfs file system to detect and prevent various race conditions that can occur when deleting a qgroup (quota group) while writing quotas. The tests cover scenarios where an extent's owner reference outlives its subvolume, leading to potential underflows or transaction aborts. The patch aims to ensure correct quota accounting by rejecting qgroup deletion requests in these situations.
